source_paper_title: Optimal Budget Aggregation with Star-Shaped Preference Domains
context:
Page 15, Section 5.2 (\(\ell_\infty\) preferences), after Theorem 2 (and before Section 6).

We conjecture that the incompatibility of efficiency, strategyproofness, and weak fairness conditions holds for \(\ell_p\) disutilities for any \(1 \le p \le \infty\), when \(m \ge 3\) and \(n \ge 3\).
title: Incompatibility of efficiency, strategyproofness, and proportionality for \(\ell_p\) preferences when \(m\ge 3\) and \(n\ge 3\)
background_latex:
- Let \(N=[n]=\{1,\dots,n\}\) be a set of agents and \(M=[m]=\{1,\dots,m\}\) a set of alternatives.
- Let \(\Delta^m:=\{q\in\mathbb{R}^m_{\ge 0}:\sum_{j\in M} q_j=1\}\) be the simplex of distributions over \(M\).
- Each agent \(i\in N\) reports a peak (ideal distribution) \(p_i\in\Delta^m\).
- Fix \(p\in[1,\infty]\). Define the \(\ell_p\) distance on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) by \(\|x\|_p=(\sum_{j\in M}|x_j|^p)^{1/p}\) for \(p<\infty\), and \(\|x\|_\infty=\max_{j\in M}|x_j|\).
- Under \(\ell_p\) preferences, agent \(i\)'s utility for outcome \(q\in\Delta^m\) is \(u_i(q):=-\|p_i-q\|_p\).
- A (tops-only) mechanism is a function \(f:(\Delta^m)^n\to\Delta^m\) mapping a peak profile \(P=(p_1,\dots,p_n)\) to an outcome \(f(P)\in\Delta^m\).
- Efficiency (Pareto efficiency): an outcome \(q\in\Delta^m\) for profile \(P\) is efficient if there is no \(q'\in\Delta^m\) with \(u_i(q')\ge u_i(q)\) for all \(i\in N\) and \(u_{i^*}(q')>u_{i^*}(q)\) for some \(i^*\in N\).
- Strategyproofness: \(f\) is strategyproof if for every profile \(P\), every agent \(i\), and every misreport \(p_i'\in\Delta^m\), letting \(P'=(p_i',p_{-i})\), one has \(u_i(f(P))\ge u_i(f(P'))\).
- A profile \(P\) is single-minded if \(p_{i,j}\in\{0,1\}\) for all \(i\in N\), \(j\in M\).
- Proportionality: \(f\) is proportional if for every single-minded profile \(P\), for every \(j\in M\), \(f(P)_j=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i\in N} p_{i,j}\).
problem_definition_latex:
Prove or disprove the following conjecture. For every \(p\in[1,\infty]\), whenever \(m\ge 3\) and \(n\ge 3\), there does not exist a mechanism \(f:(\Delta^m)^n\to\Delta^m\) that simultaneously satisfies:
- efficiency (Pareto efficiency) with respect to \(\ell_p\) utilities \(u_i(q)=-\|p_i-q\|_p\),
- strategyproofness with respect to these utilities, and
- proportionality (agreement with the coordinate-wise average on every single-minded profile).
latex:
- Let \(N=[n]=\{1,\dots,n\}\) be a set of agents and \(M=[m]=\{1,\dots,m\}\) a set of alternatives.
- Let \(\Delta^m:=\{q\in\mathbb{R}^m_{\ge 0}:\sum_{j\in M} q_j=1\}\) be the simplex of distributions over \(M\).
- Each agent \(i\in N\) reports a peak (ideal distribution) \(p_i\in\Delta^m\).
- Fix \(p\in[1,\infty]\). Define the \(\ell_p\) distance on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) by \(\|x\|_p=(\sum_{j\in M}|x_j|^p)^{1/p}\) for \(p<\infty\), and \(\|x\|_\infty=\max_{j\in M}|x_j|\).
- Under \(\ell_p\) preferences, agent \(i\)'s utility for outcome \(q\in\Delta^m\) is \(u_i(q):=-\|p_i-q\|_p\).
- A (tops-only) mechanism is a function \(f:(\Delta^m)^n\to\Delta^m\) mapping a peak profile \(P=(p_1,\dots,p_n)\) to an outcome \(f(P)\in\Delta^m\).
- Efficiency (Pareto efficiency): an outcome \(q\in\Delta^m\) for profile \(P\) is efficient if there is no \(q'\in\Delta^m\) with \(u_i(q')\ge u_i(q)\) for all \(i\in N\) and \(u_{i^*}(q')>u_{i^*}(q)\) for some \(i^*\in N\).
- Strategyproofness: \(f\) is strategyproof if for every profile \(P\), every agent \(i\), and every misreport \(p_i'\in\Delta^m\), letting \(P'=(p_i',p_{-i})\), one has \(u_i(f(P))\ge u_i(f(P'))\).
- A profile \(P\) is single-minded if \(p_{i,j}\in\{0,1\}\) for all \(i\in N\), \(j\in M\).
- Proportionality: \(f\) is proportional if for every single-minded profile \(P\), for every \(j\in M\), \(f(P)_j=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i\in N} p_{i,j}\).

Prove or disprove the following conjecture. For every \(p\in[1,\infty]\), whenever \(m\ge 3\) and \(n\ge 3\), there does not exist a mechanism \(f:(\Delta^m)^n\to\Delta^m\) that simultaneously satisfies:
- efficiency (Pareto efficiency) with respect to \(\ell_p\) utilities \(u_i(q)=-\|p_i-q\|_p\),
- strategyproofness with respect to these utilities, and
- proportionality (agreement with the coordinate-wise average on every single-minded profile).
